checks.a_analysis: The HTML document follows the logical structure and section order of the source PDF. It includes a responsive navigation menu (table of contents) and a skip-link for accessibility, which are expected enhancements. The primary headings (Sections 1-4) and sub-headings are placed appropriately, maintaining the visual flow of information from the original document. The shift from a fixed landscape PDF to a responsive web layout is a standard transformation and does not compromise the organization of content.

The tables in the HTML document are well-rendered using standard semantic table elements (<table>, <thead>, <tbody>, etc.) with clearly defined borders. The complex pricing tables in Section 2, which feature split vendor options for Intel and AMD models, are handled correctly to maintain data relationships. The technical configuration tables in Section 3 are dense but remain readable and structured correctly within responsive containers to prevent overflow issues on smaller screens. Headers are distinct from body content.

The meaningful image found in the source PDF, specifically the NCDIT logo on page 1, is present in the HTML document as a base64 encoded image with appropriate alt text. There are no missing meaningful images or broken image placeholders.

A review of the HTML output reveals no duplication of genuine body content. Section titles and headings appear in the navigation menu and once in the main body, which is consistent with accessible design standards. Tables and paragraphs appear only where intended based on the source PDF.
